paper_title: Molecular Phylogenetic Analysis of Mayamaea sp. JPCC CTDA0820

methods.description: Describes the process of screening a collection of diatom strains for cold tolerance and oleaginous phenotype, culturing them, and identifying the most promising strain (JPCC CTDA0820) using a BODIPY staining assay.

steps.description: Screening study to obtain cold-tolerant diatoms that have an oleaginous phenotype. Among the 70 diatom strains that were culturable in the half concentrated f-medium (f/2 medium) at 10 • C, strain JPCC CTDA0820 showed the most obvious oleaginous phenotype.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen diatom strains for culturability in f/2 medium at 10C and potential oleaginous phenotype.
outputs: List of culturable diatom strains at 10°C
Identification of strains with potential oleaginous phenotype

steps.description: Oleaginous phenotype confirmed by the BODIPY505/515 staining assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform BODIPY505/515 staining assay to confirm oleaginous phenotype.
outputs: Staining results indicating lipid content/oleaginous phenotype
Confirmation of the most oleaginous strain (JPCC CTDA0820)

methods.description: Describes the detailed procedure for identifying a newly isolated diatom strain (JPCC CTDA0820) based on its 18S rDNA sequence, including DNA extraction, PCR amplification, sequencing, multiple sequence alignment, and maximum likelihood phylogenetic tree construction.

steps.description: The cells were incubated in deionized water at 100 • C for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Lyse diatom cells by incubation in hot deionized water.

steps.description: The 18S rDNA was amplified by using a polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and the universal primer: forward primer, 5 -GGTGA TCCTG CCAGT AGTCA TATGC TTG-3 and reverse primer, 5 -GATCC TTCCG CAGGT TCACC TACGG AAACC-3 . PCR reactions were carried out using PrimeSTAR Max DNA polymerase (TaKaRa Bio Inc., Shiga, Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCR amplification of 18S rDNA using universal primers and PrimeSTAR Max DNA polymerase.

steps.description: Sequences of the 18S rDNA were determined by using the Applied Biosystems 3130xl Genetic Analyzer (Thermo Fisher scientific K.K., Kanagawa, Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: Sequence amplified 18S rDNA using a genetic analyzer.

steps.description: The 18S rDNA sequence was compared to the 18S rDNA sequences derived from other diatoms by using the multiple sequence alignment program CLUSTAL W [35].
steps.searchable_summary: Perform multiple sequence alignment of 18S rDNA sequences using CLUSTAL W.

steps.description: The maximum likelihood (ML) method was used to determine the 18S rDNA phylogeny. ML analysis was implemented by using MEGA5.2 software (Biodesign Institute, Tempe, AZ, USA) as previously reported [36].
steps.searchable_summary: Construct a phylogenetic tree using the Maximum Likelihood method in MEGA5.2 software.
Perform bootstrap analysis with 1000 replicates to assess tree branch support.
outputs: Bootstrap support values for tree branches

steps.description: Gaps were treated as missing data.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure phylogenetic analysis to treat alignment gaps as missing data.
outputs: Analysis results where gaps are handled as missing data
